citations.description: Epic ticket show_ticket result: status = in_progress (not closed), confirming not all children are closed.

Session log b151 last comment (timestamp 1784159575574283001): '6168 (F3 build-once + hatch_build None-commit fix): change 719 MERGED... Completion-verifier close FAILED on 2 AC-literalness gaps... Fix pushed as change 725 (awaiting votes). NEXT: land 725, then rebar transition … closed. fd84 (F1 release-source auth + version lockstep): OPEN, NOT started.'

findings.criterion: All five child stories are closed.
findings.detail: The epic's own status is `in_progress` (not closed), which under the project's policy means not all five child stories are closed. The session-log ticket (b151) explicitly records the handoff state as: 21dd ✓ closed, 36c7 ✓ closed, 08a8 ✓ closed, 6168 change merged but completion-verifier close FAILED on AC gaps (change 725 pushed, awaiting votes), fd84 OPEN / NOT started. Although the fd84 implementation code (release.yml authorize job, scripts/release_guards.py, fd84 tests) is present in the repository, the ticket status of the epic confirms not all five child story tickets have been closed.
findings.remediation: Close the remaining child stories: (1) land change 725, complete 6168's close gate, close the 6168 ticket; (2) claim story fd84, implement and land it through the Gerrit two-vote gate, close the fd84 ticket; then close this epic.

citations.description: Session log b151 last comment: 'fd84 (F1 release-source auth + version lockstep): OPEN, NOT started. Plan-review attestation is STALE (material changed + main advanced) -> re-review needed before claim.'
Session log b151 comment: '719 (6168) green after ast remediation, SUBMITTED -> MERGED. 4/5 landed.' and 'change 725 (awaiting votes)' — 6168 close-gap change 725 not yet merged at handoff.

findings.criterion: Each child story's change has landed on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`).
findings.detail: The session log records Gerrit merge confirmations for 21dd (ch703+705), 36c7 (ch704+705), and 08a8 (ch709). For 6168, change 719 was merged (LLM-Review+1/Verified+1 confirmed in session log) but a follow-up change 725 was pushed and awaiting votes at handoff. For fd84, the session log marks it 'OPEN, NOT started' — no Gerrit change was pushed, so no LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 votes can have been cast on it. The code is present in the repository but its pathway through the two-vote gate is not demonstrable from the codebase or the session log.
findings.remediation: Record the Gerrit change IDs and vote timestamps for 6168's follow-up change 725 and for fd84 as comments on the respective child tickets, confirming LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 votes.

citations.description: Session log b151 last comment: 'fd84 (F1 release-source auth + version lockstep): OPEN, NOT started. Plan-review attestation is STALE (material changed + main advanced) -> re-review needed before claim.' — no plan-review dispositions recorded on fd84 ticket.

findings.criterion: Each child story's plan-review advisory findings are recorded with an explicit Accept/Reject disposition and a one-line rationale (as a comment on the child ticket), and every Accepted finding is reflected in the child's description before that child's change receives its Gerrit votes.
findings.detail: This criterion requires checking each child story's ticket for the advisory disposition comments. Without the full child ticket IDs I cannot read them directly, but the session log (b151) confirms dispositions were recorded for 21dd ('21dd advisory dispositions recorded as comment — 7 accepted/reflected, 10 rejected') and 08a8 ('advisories dispositioned'). However, for fd84, the ticket is OPEN and NOT started — no plan-review has been completed and no advisory dispositions recorded. For 6168, the session log notes a re-review was required and 'AC extending test_release_workflow_pins.py' was added, but whether all advisory dispositions were formally recorded on the fd84 ticket cannot be verified. Because fd84 is open and unstarted, its plan-review advisory disposition record is absent.
findings.remediation: Complete the plan-review for fd84, record Accept/Reject dispositions with rationales as a comment on the fd84 child ticket, reflect accepted findings in the ticket description, then land the change with Gerrit votes.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
